item.title: I Was Staring At Code That Looked Fine — JFR Said Optimise Every Line
item.description:
I Was Staring At Code That Looked Fine 🔗

I’m an engineer at Hevo, and a big part of my job is making sure our ETL pipelines are fast. As part of a benchmarking exercise on the pipeline engine, I was profiling ingestion, mapping, and load separately to find out exactly where time was going. The destination mapper stood out — it’s the component responsible for converting every field of every row from our internal type system into destination-specific strings for CSV output.

item.title: Benchmarking Hevo’s Pipeline Engine
item.description:
At Hevo, I recently worked on an initiative around benchmarking and optimising our pipeline’s performance. We’ve published it as a public technical blog.

The post walks through how pipeline performance can be evaluated and why benchmarking matters when teams care about throughput, latency, and reliability in data movement.

If you work with data pipelines, ETL/ELT systems, or large-scale ingestion workloads, this is a useful read:

Benchmarking Hevo Pipeline Performance
